Vince: Did you feel that? Like someone’s watching us from just out of sight.

Lena: Don’t say that… I felt it too, but I thought it was just my nerves.

Vince: The streets are too quiet. It feels wrong.

Lena: Yeah… like the silence is listening back.

Vince: I swear I keep seeing movement when I turn around.

Lena: Same. But when I look, there’s nothing there.

Vince: We’re being watched, Lena. I’m sure of it.

Lena: I don’t like how sure you sound… because I believe you.

Vince: It started when we entered this district.

Lena: Or when something in this district noticed us.

Vince: Should we go back?

Lena: I don’t think ‘back’ is safe either anymore.

Vince: Every window feels like an eye right now.

Lena: Stop… now I’m seeing it too.

Vince: Whatever this is, it knows we know.

Lena: And I think that made it worse.

Vince: I heard footsteps behind us, but no one was there.

Lena: Don’t say that too loud… it might answer.

Vince: The streetlights keep flickering when we stop moving.

Lena: Like they only work when we’re being watched.

Vince: Did that shadow just move differently than the others?

Lena: I noticed it too… and I wish I didn’t.

Vince: I turned around for one second… and something changed.

Lena: I felt it. Like the air reset itself.

Vince: Why does it feel like the buildings are leaning in?

Lena: They always were… we just didn’t notice before.

Vince: Someone whispered my name when no one was there.

Lena: Don’t repeat it. It might like hearing it.

Vince: The reflection in the glass didn’t copy me right.

Lena: Don’t look again. Seriously.

Vince: We’re not alone here.

Lena: We haven’t been alone for a while.

Vince: I swear something is walking just out of sync with us.

Lena: Like it’s waiting for us to notice it fully.

Vince: The silence here feels heavy.

Lena: Heavy like something sitting inside it.

Vince: I blinked and the alley looked closer.

Lena: That’s not how space is supposed to work.

Vince: I think something is following our attention.

Lena: Then we should stop paying attention.

Vince: Why do all the windows go dark when we look at them?

Lena: Because they don’t want to be seen seeing us.

Vince: Something just mimicked my walking pattern.

Lena: Don’t test it again.

Vince: I feel like I’m being copied.

Lena: I feel like I’m being observed copying you.

Vince: The street behind us keeps changing when we turn back.

Lena: So we don’t turn back anymore.

Vince: I saw a figure standing where I was a second ago.

Lena: That’s not possible… unless it’s faster than us.

Vince: The air feels like it’s waiting for permission.

Lena: Permission from what?

Vince: Something is learning how we move.

Lena: Then we stop moving the same way.

Vince: I think the district is alive.

Lena: Or worse… aware.

Vince: Did the street just get quieter when we stopped talking?

Lena: Yeah… it did.

Vince: I don’t like how it reacts to us.

Lena: Then stop reacting back.

Vince: I looked up and every window was facing us.

Lena: That’s not a coincidence anymore.

Vince: Something is pacing us.

Lena: I can feel it too.

Vince: The lights blinked like they were counting.

Lena: Counting what?

Vince: Us.

Lena: Don’t say it like that…

Vince: I think it knows when we notice it.

Lena: Then we should pretend not to.

Vince: There’s something between the sounds here.

Lena: Like gaps that don’t belong?

Vince: The walls feel closer than before.

Lena: Or we’re just being pushed inward.

Vince: I saw movement in a reflection that wasn’t ours.

Lena: That’s the second time today…

Vince: I think it’s getting closer.

Lena: Or we’re getting easier to see.

Vince: If something is watching us… why doesn’t it show itself?

Lena: Because it doesn’t need to.

Vince: I don’t trust the way the street ends anymore.

Lena: It didn’t always end there.

Vince: I feel like we’re being studied.

Lena: Not studied… evaluated.

Vince: The district feels wrong tonight.

Lena: It always does… we just notice more now.

Vince: Something is walking behind us again.

Lena: Don’t turn around.

Vince: Too late… I already did.

Lena: …and?
Vince: Nothing. Which is worse.
Lena: Yeah… it means it noticed you noticing.

Vince: I think it’s not outside anymore.

Lena: Don’t finish that sentence.

Vince: We should leave.

Lena: I don’t think it lets people leave once they see it.

Vince: It’s watching us even when we’re still.

Lena: Especially when we’re still.
